item.title: There can be no Labour revival if Starmer remains
item.description: The prime minister still sounds like an opposition leader

item.title: Trump’s war has given China an economic opening
item.description: Beijing could turn instability in the Middle East into a strategic gain

item.title: A Keynesian solution to global imbalances
item.description: Economists have been proposing fixes to structural problems created by an international reserve currency since the 1940s

item.title: Buckle up: plane tickets could still get much more expensive
item.description: Airlines have managed to avoid greater turbulence because jet fuel hasn’t actually run short — yet

item.title: The ‘surname ceiling’ holding back family companies
item.description: Many of the brightest workers steer clear of such businesses because promotions are scarcer and rewards lower

item.title: America’s right and left have perfected each other’s tricks
item.description: Both extremes now have an affinity for cancel culture and conspiracy theories

item.title: The narrow ceasefire rally
item.description: Little love for the S&P 500’s gains

item.title: The glory of low expectations
item.description: They are the key to happiness — and not a matter of choice

item.title: Hendrix and the curse of the charismatic frontman
item.description: A recent court case over the rights to the rock star’s catalogue highlights the struggles of musicians seeking their due

item.title: WHSmith’s new owner makes a contrarian bet on the UK high street
item.description: Modella Capital plans to cut as many as 150 WHSmith stores out of the 480 it acquired and bring in new goods and services

item.title: Standard Life and CVC square the circle on defined-benefit pensions
item.description: There are more than £1tn of assets sitting in UK retirement schemes that could be picked up in risk transfer deals

item.title: Who are the tastemakers now?
item.description: Few influencers have the same cultural gravitas as the late American heiress Bunny Mellon

item.title: We are living in the age of the middleman
item.description: Special envoys are the new peacemakers precisely because they are unburdened by diplomatic expertise

item.title: The places where we seek wisdom
item.description: In a world seemingly obsessed with youth, we can easily miss the gifts that come from our elders

item.title: Why are foreigners flocking to make wine in England?
item.description: Producers from Burgundy, and further afield, are getting increasingly experimental

item.title: Romano’s and Simpson’s-in-the-Strand: A legendary building restored to its prime
item.description: Rejoice! Jeremy King has brought an old-fashioned (in the best way) destination back to life

item.title: Goldman’s big board bet
item.description: Bank’s management committee has swelled to a much larger size than those of its competitors

item.title: The new politics may be more like the old politics than we assume
item.description: Our electoral system will force consolidation within the left and right blocs

item.title: How politicians should talk to bond markets
item.description: It might be annoying that money is not free but a combative approach with investors is not a great choice

item.title: Keir Starmer and Britain’s new politics of instability
item.description: Labour’s failures in office have opened the way to Reform and other insurgent parties

item.title: While AI pumps up portfolios, some companies sound the alarm
item.description: The clout of Big Tech groups is obscuring the precarious state of others

item.title: Welcome to the era of five-party politics
item.description: Widespread disillusionment with Labour and the Tories is understandable

item.title: Markets are banking on the ‘Bliss trade’
item.description: A belief in resilience underwritten by big lasting state support is keeping stocks high

item.title: ‘Savvy the Squirrel’ has missed a trick
item.description: If there’s one place cash does not belong, it’s in a Junior Cash Isa — so why doesn’t the government promote investing for your children?

item.title: How can I speak ill of the dead?
item.description: Three tricks for avoiding cancellation
